title: hydrophyllum canadense
intro: Hydrophyllum canadense, also known as Indian or Canadian squawroot, is a perennial plant that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae) and is native to North America. The medicinal properties of Hydrophyllum canadense include its ability to soothe digestive issues and its antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation, as well as its ability to alleviate symptoms of diarrhea and dysentery. Hydrophyllum canadense can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of ailments.
